keyFindings.title: Reference phase is read-only and artifact-scoped
keyFindings.summary: The phase explicitly creates research outputs and lineage packets while excluding merge execution or runtime/workflow modifications.

keyFindings.title: Forward runtime-to-workflows coupling is explicitly mapped
keyFindings.summary: Class A sites preserve hop count while deleting the old workflows segment after runtime nesting.

keyFindings.title: Reverse workflows-to-runtime coupling needs different hop-count repair
keyFindings.summary: Class B is a minus-one-hop rename problem and should not be handled by blind replacement.

keyFindings.title: System-spec-kit tooling-borrow repair is load-bearing
keyFindings.summary: The runtime typecheck and system-spec-kit council test paths cross the move boundary and belong in child 002 validation.

keyFindings.title: Advisor projections and ledgers are migration-critical
keyFindings.summary: The rename must cover advisor code constants, generated projections, tests, routing corpora, and approved-divergence ledgers.

keyFindings.title: Command contract migration must regenerate compiled outputs
keyFindings.summary: Source YAML and router markdown should be edited, then compiled contracts regenerated rather than hand-edited.

keyFindings.title: System-spec-kit is a high-risk migration consumer
keyFindings.summary: Package scripts, vitest globs, workflow docs, and reducer tests still reference old deep-loop paths.

keyFindings.title: Grandfather examples require semantic update, not just replacement
keyFindings.summary: The `system-deep-loop` name creates a command-prefix exception that parent-skill examples need to state explicitly.

keyFindings.title: Fanout retry exhaustion does not currently invoke fallback resolution
keyFindings.summary: `fanout-pool.cjs` retries and then settles retry-exhausted failures; the optional plan adds the missing resolveFallback call site.

keyFindings.title: GLM and MiMo are separate active pools, but no fallback edge is configured
keyFindings.summary: The model profiles show `glm-5.2` and `mimo-v2.5-pro` active with distinct pools and both `fallback_target: null`.

keyFindings.title: Fallback wiring should remain optional unless scope changes
keyFindings.summary: Child 004 is explicitly optional and awaiting operator decision; it should be called out but not used to block child 002/003.

resolvedQuestions.answer: Child 002 covers both coupling directions, tooling-borrow, metadata consolidation, and validation gates.
Child 003 covers advisor, command, docs, tests, ledgers, graph metadata, and examples with dependency ordering.
Automatic GLM-to-MiMo fallback is not currently live.
Fallback wiring remains optional unless the operator expands scope.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Treat GLM-to-MiMo fallback as already active during fan-out
ruledOutDirections.reason: Registry fallback targets are null and retry exhaustion does not call the fallback router.

ruledOutDirections.approach: Block structural rename on fallback wiring
